73925.subject: Sam Altman sued for "hurting" kids
73925.body:
----------
######                      [Sign Up](http://utm.genai.works/r/gg2ubr5) | [Collaborate with us](https://6hhfxazcbpc.typeform.com/to/uXIl0IFH?utm_source=atlas&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=general_2026_05_11&utm_content=banner_post)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** Busy day for AI news. Florida became the first state to take OpenAI to court, and it wants Sam Altman to pay out of his own pocket. Meta's AI help desk spent months handing Instagram accounts to anyone who asked it the right way. And Anthropic filed to go public on the back of the fastest revenue run any company has ever posted. The checks and the lawsuits are both getting bigger.

———————————————————————————

**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **OpenAI in court:** What happens when a state tries to make ChatGPT legally responsible for a kid's safety?

* **Meta's open door:** How did a few polite questions to an AI bot unlock someone's Instagram?

* **Anthropic's IPO:** Can the fastest revenue ramp in history hold up in public markets?

* **Free code guard:** How do you get Anthropic's new security plugin running in two minutes?


----------

----------
## [Florida sues OpenAI and wants Altman to pay personally](https://www.ft.com/content/7998db0a-22d1-4b80-b861-2e8b2448e97f?syn-25a6b1a6=1)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3db240e7-324f-44a9-8f92-b047f0b87fd7/Beehiiv_01_-_Jun_2__1_.png?t=1780390070)
Caption: 

Florida became the first state to take OpenAI to court Monday, and it's going straight at the CEO. 

Attorney General James Uthmeier accused the company of deceptive trade practices, negligence and product liability, and asked a judge to hold Sam Altman personally liable for the harm he blames on ChatGPT.

The complaint reads like a catalog of worst cases.

A chatbot that coached a mass shooter, pushed minors toward suicide and hooked kids with no real age check or parental controls. 

OpenAI says it built protections for minors, including age prediction and parental monitoring, and admits none of that "will bring a child back."

The Attorney General doesn't want a fine. He wants OpenAI to **rewrite how the product works**, and he expects other states to follow. 

The suit builds on his criminal probe into last year's Florida State University shooting. If a court rules that a chatbot's words make it a defective product, every AI company's rulebook for minors changes at once.


----------



----------
## [How to build your first application with AI](https://utm.genai.works/r/a7em0vh)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,oner

73924.subject: Reminder: Acceptance of Autopay Mobility Terms of Use Required
73924.body:
Autopay [https://mailing.autopay.eu/email_marketing_click.html?x=a62e&co=Sn3P&lc=oN&mc=o&s=45ty&u=t&z=gwVptWF&] 
Reminder: Acceptance of Autopay Mobility Terms of Use Required





Dear Autopay customer,

We would like to inform you about the new terms of use for Autopay Mobility, covering changes to billing rules and an expanded range of available services.






What do you need to do?
To continue using paid services in the Autopay app, please accept the new Price List.
Until your consent is provided, automatic services (e.g. motorway tolls, automatic parking) will be suspended.

I accept the new terms [https://app.autopay.pl/regulation-acceptance/8e52ddc72cb34af597e041e6e01c8bae] 




What is changing?

We are expanding the range of services
We are launching city parking payments in over 100 cities in Poland via the Autopay app.
Pay for parking with one click - no cash, no parking meters, and no need to remember to extend your parking time. All charges and billing history will be available in one place together with your other Autopay services.

We are standardizing billing rules for all users
From that date, service fees will be charged regardless of the number of vehicles added. The current rules that excluded fees for accounts with one vehicle will no longer apply.

We are adjusting the invoice delivery method for business users
We have introduced changes to how invoices are delivered to business users so that the process complies with applicable legal regulations and the requirements of the National e-Invoicing System (KSeF).





The full content of the new terms of use can be found in the Terms and Conditions [https://mailing.autopay.eu/email_marketing_click.html?x=a62e&co=Sn3P&lc=oM&mc=o&s=45ty&u=t&z=gWnasRl&] and Price List [https://mailing.autopay.eu/email_marketing_click.html?x=a62e&co=Sn3P&lc=oR&mc=o&s=45ty&u=t&z=gAS63eX&].
Please also note that in connection with the change to the Autopay terms of use, as a consumer you have the right to terminate the agreement in accordance with the rules set out in the Terms and Conditions.
If you have any questions about these changes, please visit the Help section or contact our support team.





Best regards,
Autopay Team


This message has been sent in accordance with the Autopay terms and conditions, by Autopay Mobility Sp. z o.o. with its registered office in Sopot, ul. Powstańców Warszawy 6, 81-718 Sopot, registered in the District Court Gdańsk-Północ, 8th Commercial Division of the National Court Register under No. 0000794787, NIP 527-28-98-696, REGON 383879060. Share capital PLN 19,296,050.00. If you are not the intended recipient of this message, please notify us and delete the message without opening any attachments.



Aby się wypisać przejdź do:
https://mailing.autopay.eu/unsubscribe.html?x=a62e&co=Sn3P&m=tN&mc=o&s=45ty&u=t&z=g8wL81x&
Aby zmienić swoje dane kontaktowe przejdź do:
https://mailing.autopay.eu/change_details.html?x=a62e&co=Sn3P&m=tN&s=45ty&u=t&z=gvgRG8i&

73916.subject: Strategy's First Bitcoin Sale Since 2022 Trips a Polymarket Oracle
73916.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

* **[Strategy](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/strategy-sells-32-bitcoin-fund-preferred-dividends-first-sale-since-2022)**[ sells 32 BTC, its first disclosed sale since 2022](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/strategy-sells-32-bitcoin-fund-preferred-dividends-first-sale-since-2022)

* **[$60M Polymarket](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/usd85m-polymarket-dispute-over-strategy-s-may-bitcoin-sale-puts-uma-s-token-voting-oracle-on)**[ contract on Strategy's sale heads to UMA dispute](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/usd85m-polymarket-dispute-over-strategy-s-may-bitcoin-sale-puts-uma-s-token-voting-oracle-on)

* **[Gnosis Pay](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/gnosis-pay-hit-by-delay-module-exploit-as-gnosis-pledges-to-cover-user-losses)**[ hit by 'Delay Module' exploit; Gnosis covers losses](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/gnosis-pay-hit-by-delay-module-exploit-as-gnosis-pledges-to-cover-user-losses)

* **[Anchorage Digital](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/anchorage-digital-coordinated-multiparty-settlement-institutional-defi)**[ opens federal-charter settlement for non-custodial DeFi](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/anchorage-digital-coordinated-multiparty-settlement-institutional-defi)

* **[Binance](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/binance-opens-us-stock-trading-non-us-users-bstocks-bnb-chain)**[ opens US stock trading to non-US users, plans bStocks](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/binance-opens-us-stock-trading-non-us-users-bstocks-bnb-chain)

**Strategy** filed an 8-K Monday showing the [disposal of 32 BTC between May 26 and May 31 at an average $77,135 a coin](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/strategy-sells-32-bitcoin-fund-preferred-dividends-first-sale-since-2022), with proceeds earmarked for distributions on its STRC preferred stock. Small in absolute terms, the sale matters because it is the first disclosed Strategy disposal since the December 2022 tax-loss harvest. [The pivot we flagged last week](https://thedefiant.io/newsletter/ethereum-s-stake-queue-outpaces-exits-53-000-to-1) — Saylor adding bond exposure to a portfolio that had been a pure Bitcoin-treasury anchor — stopped being theoretical.

The same news immediately broke the [$60 million Polymarket contract](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/usd85m-polymarket-dispute-over-strategy-s-may-bitcoin-sale-puts-uma-s-token-voting-oracle-on) that bet on it. The market question — 'Will Strategy sell any bitcoin before May 31?' — was disputed twice and is now in front of **UMA** tokenholders. The narrow question is whether a 32-BTC disposal earmarked for preferred-stock dividends counts as 'selling bitcoin' under the contract's terms. The strategic question is whether UMA's token-voting oracle can settle high-stakes factual claims when the underlying event is itself ambiguous.

**Read more below!**

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=re
